slides.title: The nutrient that *widens your arteries*.
slides.sub: It comes down to nitric oxide — the molecule that relaxes your blood vessels. May lower blood pressure and lift your endurance.

slides.title: Nitrates become nitric oxide
slides.body: Your body converts *dietary nitrates* into nitric oxide, which signals blood vessels to relax and widen.

slides.title: Wider vessels, lower pressure
slides.body: Relaxed vessels mean easier blood flow. Studies link nitrate-rich diets to *modestly lower blood pressure*.

slides.title: Eat the deep greens
slides.body: Beets, arugula, spinach and rocket are the richest sources. A *daily handful of greens* adds up.

slides.title: Cheaper than pre-workout
slides.body: More nitric oxide can mean better oxygen use. Beet juice may boost *stamina by a few percent*.

slides.title: Time it right
slides.body: Effects peak *2–3 hours* after eating. A beet salad before a walk or workout stacks the benefit.

slides.title: Eat for *steadier energy*.
slides.body: Vibe Health logs your meals and ties what you eat to your daily energy and Health Score — so the wins get obvious.

caption:
Your blood vessels have a natural dilator, and it lives in your salad bowl. 🥬

Dietary nitrates turn into nitric oxide, beets and leafy greens are the richest sources, and effects peak 2–3 hours after you eat. Save this for your next grocery run. 🔖

Vibe Health ties what you eat to how much energy you actually have → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Vegetable That Widens Your Arteries
